description_fr: États, fleuves, villes, parcs nationaux. Découvrez les États-Unis.
description_en: States, rivers, cities, national parks. Discover the United States.

questions.question_en: How many states does the USA have?
questions.explanation_fr: 50 États depuis 1959 (Hawaii et Alaska ajoutés cette année-là).
questions.explanation_en: 50 states since 1959 (Hawaii and Alaska added that year).

questions.question_fr: Quel est le plus grand État américain ?
questions.question_en: Which is the largest US state?
questions.explanation_fr: L'Alaska (1,7 million km²) est plus de 2 fois plus grand que le Texas.
questions.explanation_en: Alaska (1.7M km²) is more than twice the size of Texas.

questions.explanation_fr: Faux. Washington D.C. est un district fédéral, pas un État (statehood en débat).
questions.explanation_en: False. Washington D.C. is a federal district, not a state (statehood debated).

questions.question_fr: Quel fleuve traverse le Grand Canyon ?
questions.question_en: Which river runs through the Grand Canyon?
questions.explanation_fr: Le fleuve Colorado a creusé le Grand Canyon (Arizona) sur 6 millions d'années.
questions.explanation_en: The Colorado River carved the Grand Canyon (Arizona) over 6 million years.

questions.question_fr: Combien y a-t-il de monts sur le Mount Rushmore ?
questions.question_en: How many faces are carved on Mount Rushmore?
questions.explanation_fr: 4 présidents : Washington, Jefferson, Lincoln, T. Roosevelt. Sculpté 1927-1941.
questions.explanation_en: 4 presidents: Washington, Jefferson, Lincoln, T. Roosevelt. Carved 1927-1941.

questions.question_en: The capital of the United States is ___.
questions.explanation_fr: Washington D.C. (District of Columbia) capitale depuis 1800.
questions.explanation_en: Washington D.C. (District of Columbia) has been the capital since 1800.

questions.question_fr: Quelle ville américaine est surnommée 'The Big Apple' ?
questions.question_en: Which US city is nicknamed 'The Big Apple'?
questions.explanation_fr: New York City, surnommée la Big Apple depuis les années 1920 (turfistes).
questions.explanation_en: New York City, nicknamed Big Apple since the 1920s (horse racing).

questions.explanation_fr: Hawaii est un archipel volcanique : Big Island, Maui, O'ahu, Kauaʻi, etc.
questions.explanation_en: Hawaii is a volcanic archipelago: Big Island, Maui, Oʻahu, Kauaʻi, etc.

questions.explanation_fr: Hollywood est un quartier de Los Angeles, Californie.
questions.explanation_en: Hollywood is a neighborhood of Los Angeles, California.

questions.question_fr: Quel est le plus long fleuve des États-Unis ?
questions.question_en: What is the longest river in the USA?
questions.explanation_fr: Le Missouri (3 767 km) est plus long que le Mississippi (3 730 km), mais ils se rejoignent.
questions.explanation_en: The Missouri (3,767 km) is longer than the Mississippi (3,730 km), but they join.

questions.question_fr: Le plus grand parc national américain s'appelle ___.
questions.question_en: The largest US national park is ___.
questions.explanation_fr: Wrangell-St Elias (Alaska) est plus grand. Yellowstone (Wyoming, 1872) le plus ancien.
questions.explanation_en: Wrangell-St Elias (Alaska) is larger. Yellowstone (Wyoming, 1872) is the oldest.

questions.question_fr: La Statue de la Liberté a été un cadeau de la France.
questions.question_en: The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France.
questions.explanation_fr: Cadeau du peuple français aux USA pour le centenaire de l'indépendance, inaugurée en 1886.
questions.explanation_en: Gift from France to the USA for the independence centennial, inaugurated in 1886.

questions.question_fr: Quel État a la forme d'une botte ?
questions.question_en: Which US state is shaped like a boot?
questions.explanation_fr: Le Michigan (péninsule supérieure et inférieure) est souvent comparé à une mitaine.
questions.explanation_en: Michigan (upper and lower peninsulas) is often compared to a mitten.

questions.question_fr: Quelle ville américaine est surnommée 'la Cité du Vent' ?
questions.question_en: Which US city is nicknamed 'the Windy City'?
questions.explanation_fr: Chicago, à cause des vents violents du lac Michigan et de sa politique 'venteuse'.
questions.explanation_en: Chicago, because of strong winds off Lake Michigan and 'windy' politics.

questions.question_fr: Le désert le plus chaud des USA est la Vallée de la ___.
questions.question_en: The hottest US desert is ___ Valley.
questions.explanation_fr: Death Valley (Californie) a enregistré 56,7°C, record mondial.
questions.explanation_en: Death Valley (California) recorded 56.7°C, a world record.
